instrument_name: Research Sexual Interests, Consent, and Context Inventory (R-SICCI)
purpose: A non-clinical, research-only item bank for studying consensual adult sexual interests, consent/communication, social context, wellbeing, and a separately governed optional sensitive thought/interest module.

administration.population: Adults age 18+ only, subject to IRB approval.
administration.mode: Self-administered computer or mobile survey with a private completion option.
administration.target_duration: 90–120 minutes, including consent, breaks, and optional module; implement progress-saving and a pause/resume feature.
administration.no_diagnosis: The instrument does not diagnose, predict criminality, or determine fitness, eligibility, or treatment.
administration.preamble: Adults vary widely in sexual interests. An interest, fantasy, or consensual adult activity is not automatically a problem. You may skip any item. Unless a question says otherwise, questions about behavior refer only to legal activity between adults who were able to choose freely and clearly consented.

modules.name: Eligibility & research administration
modules.purpose: Adult-only prescreen; privacy; recruitment / compensation; randomized method condition.

modules.name: Background & context
modules.purpose: Minimal demographic and social-context covariates; no precise location.

modules.name: General sexual and relational context
modules.purpose: Broad comparison context; not used to define normality.

modules.name: Consensual adult interest matrix
modules.purpose: 38 themes x appeal, desire, and consensual-adult experience; displayed as a matrix.

modules.name: Up to five personally relevant themes
modules.purpose: Selected-theme follow-up; display only for themes the participant endorsed.

modules.name: Identity, community & learning
modules.purpose: Identity/community as dimensions separate from practices.

modules.name: Consent, communication & boundaries
modules.purpose: Self-reported skills/resources and consent-related experiences; no event details.

modules.name: Social & environmental context
modules.purpose: Acceptance, stigma, education, support, and disclosure concerns.

modules.name: Wellbeing & personal impact
modules.purpose: Exploratory wellbeing and impact; not a clinical screen.

modules.name: Optional restricted thought/interest module
modules.purpose: Thoughts/urges only; no behavioral, target, event, date, or narrative questions.

modules.name: Quality & debrief
modules.purpose: Participant experience and non-punitive response-quality indicators.

APPEAL_0_4.label: Erotic/sexual appeal
options: I am unfamiliar with this theme

DESIRE_0_4.label: Interest in a legal, consensual adult version
options: I am unfamiliar with this theme

PRACTICE_0_4.label: Consensual adult experience
options: Lifetime experience, not in the past 12 months
A few times in the past 12 months

items.participant_prompt: Are you age 18 or older?
items.skip_logic: Terminate with neutral ineligible screen if No or Prefer not to answer.

items.participant_prompt: Are you currently in a setting where you have enough privacy to answer personal questions?
items.skip_logic: If No, offer pause/resume or reschedule. Do not terminate.
items.rationale: Privacy affects both participant welfare and response quality.

items.participant_prompt: What compensation, if any, are you receiving for participating?
items.response_options: None; course credit; fixed payment; raffle/lottery entry; other; prefer not to answer

items.participant_prompt: In what language did you complete the survey?
SYSTEM FIELD: randomized survey-method condition.
items.response_options: A=plain language; B=plain language plus normalized preamble; C=term label plus definition
items.developer_notes: Do not display this field. Randomize 1:1:1 and retain with participant record.

items.participant_prompt: How were you invited to this study?
items.response_options: University participant pool; paid online panel; community organization; social media; personal referral; other; prefer not to answer

items.participant_prompt: What is your age range?
Which best describes where you live?
items.response_options: Urban; suburban; small town; rural; prefer not to answer

items.participant_prompt: What is your highest completed education level?
items.response_options: Some high school; high school/GED; some college; associate; bachelor; graduate/professional; prefer not to answer

items.participant_prompt: What is your current employment or student status?
items.response_options: Full-time student; part-time student; employed full-time; employed part-time; not currently employed; retired; another status; prefer not to answer

items.participant_prompt: In the past 12 months, how difficult has it been to meet your basic expenses?
items.response_options: Not difficult; a little difficult; moderately difficult; very difficult; extremely difficult; prefer not to answer

items.participant_prompt: How do you currently describe your gender?
items.response_options: Woman; man; nonbinary; another identity (self-describe); prefer not to answer

items.participant_prompt: Which term or terms best describe your sexual orientation?
items.response_options: Heterosexual/straight; gay/lesbian; bisexual; pansexual; asexual-spectrum; questioning; another identity (self-describe); prefer not to answer

items.participant_prompt: Which broad region do you live in?
items.response_options: Northeast U.S.; Midwest U.S.; South U.S.; West U.S.; U.S. territory; outside U.S.; prefer not to answer

items.participant_prompt: How important are religion or spirituality in your life?
items.response_options: Not at all; slightly; moderately; very; extremely; prefer not to answer

items.participant_prompt: Which relationship agreement best describes your current situation?
items.response_options: Not currently partnered; monogamous; consensually non-monogamous; open relationship; polyamorous; relationship agreement unclear/being negotiated; another agreement; prefer not to answer

items.participant_prompt: What is your current relationship status?
items.response_options: Single; dating; partnered and not cohabiting; cohabiting; married/civil partnership; separated/divorced/widowed; another status; prefer not to answer

items.participant_prompt: What sex were you assigned at birth? This item is optional and should be included only if scientifically necessary.
items.response_options: Female; male; intersex; another response; prefer not to answer

items.participant_prompt: I can discuss sexual interests or boundaries with a partner when I choose to.
I can access sexual-health or mental-health support that I trust if I need it.
I generally feel able to explore sexual interests at my own pace.
I have sufficient privacy in my daily life to make free choices about consensual adult intimacy.
If you currently have a romantic or sexual partner, how satisfied are you with the relationship overall?
items.response_options: 0–10 numeric scale; 0=not at all satisfied; 10=extremely satisfied; not currently partnered; prefer not to answer

items.participant_prompt: Overall, how satisfied are you with your sexual life at this time?
items.response_options: 0–10 numeric scale; 0=not at all satisfied; 10=extremely satisfied

items.skip_logic: Display when TOP_THEME_05 contains an interest theme.

items.participant_prompt: When this theme involves an interpersonal role, which role best describes you?
items.response_options: Mostly leading/giving; mostly receiving/following; both or versatile; varies by situation; no role preference; not applicable; prefer not to answer
items.skip_logic: Display when TOP_THEME_05 contains an interest theme.

items.participant_prompt: Over the past 12 months, has your interest in this theme generally changed?
items.response_options: Much less interested; somewhat less interested; about the same; somewhat more interested; much more interested; unsure; prefer not to answer
items.skip_logic: Display when TOP_THEME_05 contains an interest theme.

items.participant_prompt: Have concerns about judgment, rejection, or disclosure affected how you feel about this theme?
items.response_options: Not at all; a little; moderately; a great deal; extremely; prefer not to answer
items.skip_logic: Display when TOP_THEME_05 contains an interest theme.

items.participant_prompt: I worry about being judged, rejected, or misunderstood because of my sexual interests.
I have been mocked, threatened, excluded, or treated unfairly because of my sexual interests or relationship style.
My family or household would be accepting if they knew about my sexual interests.
Growing up, I could discuss relationships, boundaries, or sexuality with a trusted adult.
I expect a health-care or mental-health professional to treat these interests respectfully.
I have avoided seeking care because I expected judgment about my sexual interests or relationship style.
I often feel isolated because I cannot safely discuss this part of myself with others.
Media, social media, or online communities have influenced how I understand sexual interests or relationships.
A current or likely partner would be accepting if they knew about my sexual interests.
People close to me would be accepting if they knew about my sexual interests.
My prior sex education addressed consent and sexual diversity in a useful way.
I have people in my life who provide practical or emotional support when I need it.
I worry that disclosure could affect employment, education, housing, custody, or another important area of life.
During the past six months, how difficult has it been to manage any sexual thoughts or urges that you personally experience as unwanted?

score_rules.inputs: For each theme: valid Appeal and/or Desire score (0–4); theme score = mean of valid items. CII = mean theme score / 4 * 100.
score_rules.eligibility: At least 70% of D-matrix Appeal/Desire cells that were displayed must be valid. Codes 97–99 are missing, not zero.
score_rules.use: Descriptive research axis; never a clinical or risk score.
score_rules.interpretation: 0–24 lower disclosed interest; 25–49 slight-to-moderate; 50–74 moderate-to-strong; 75–100 strong. These are response-scale anchors, not population percentiles or health categories.

score_rules.inputs: Number of themes with theme score >= 2.0 / number of themes with valid Appeal or Desire * 100.
score_rules.use: Describes the spread of endorsed interests across this item bank.
score_rules.interpretation: Also output a count. Suggested profile tags: 0–1 disclosed themes = no/few themes; 2–4 = focused; 5–9 = multi-theme; 10+ = broad. Do not use as a rank or health measure.

score_rules.inputs: Practice = 0–4 per theme. CAP = mean valid Practice / 4 * 100; also retain highest recency code.
score_rules.eligibility: At least 70% of displayed Practice cells valid.
score_rules.interpretation: Report a non-evaluative tag: no practice disclosed; past only; recent; occasional; regular. Never infer consent quality from this score.

score_rules.inputs: Mean of KID_IMPORTANCE, KID_COMMUNITY_ONLINE, KID_COMMUNITY_INPERSON, KID_EDUCATION, KID_TRUSTED_PEER, and KID_PARAPHERNALIA / 4 * 100.
score_rules.use: Describes identity/community connection; not required for interest or practice.
score_rules.interpretation: 0–24 low salience; 25–49 emerging; 50–74 connected; 75–100 highly salient. Add KID_NO_IDENTITY as a separate contextual tag; never score privacy as concealment or pathology.

score_rules.inputs: Mean of CON_NEGOTIATE, CON_STOP, CON_CHANGE_MIND, CON_PARTNER_RESPECT, CON_DECLINE_SAFE, CON_CAPACITY, reverse(CON_PRESSURE), CON_CHECKIN, CON_AFTERCARE, CON_RECORDING, CON_SUBSTANCE, CON_INFORMATION / 4 * 100.
score_rules.use: Self-report of skills/resources, not a safety clearance and not a predictor of offending or victimization.
score_rules.interpretation: 0–49 lower self-reported resources; 50–74 variable/developing; 75–100 higher self-reported resources. Do not present to participants as a pass/fail result.

score_rules.inputs: Retain CON_LIMIT_CROSSED, CON_UNABLE_STOP, CON_MISUNDERSTOOD, and CON_SUPPORT_SOUGHT as separate variables; no composite.
score_rules.use: Context for research and service design.
score_rules.interpretation: Do not infer legal status, perpetration, victimization, or risk from these data. Do not collect event narratives in the baseline study.

score_rules.inputs: Mean of SOC_WORKPLACE_WORRY, SOC_DISCLOSURE_WORRY, SOC_DISCRIMINATION, SOC_HEALTHCARE_AVOID, SOC_ISOLATED, DFI_SOCIAL, plus the mean of displayed TOP##_STIGMA values normalized to 0–4, then /4 *100.
score_rules.eligibility: At least 5 core variables valid; selected-theme stigma is optional and should not be required.
score_rules.use: Separates social strain from intrinsic distress.
score_rules.interpretation: Report as a continuous 0–100 scale. Do not call it shame, pathology, or maladjustment.

score_rules.inputs: Mean of DFI_INTRUSIVE, DFI_VALUES, DFI_FUNCTION, DFI_CONTROL, DFI_SAFETY, plus mean TOP##_POS_NEG mapped: No=0, Minor=1, Moderate=2, Substantial=3; omit unsure / PNTA, then /4 *100.
score_rules.eligibility: At least 4 of the 5 core DFI items valid.
score_rules.use: Research signal for self-reported unwantedness/impact; not a diagnosis.
score_rules.interpretation: Keep intrinsic and stigma-related distress separate. If DFI_SAFETY >=3 or DFI_FUNCTION >=3, show a universal, optional support-resources screen; do not alert staff or make a legal/clinical conclusion without an IRB-approved protocol.

score_rules.inputs: Mean of WB_LIFE_SAT normalized 0–4, WB_ENERGY, WB_CALM, WB_INTEREST, WB_SOCIAL, reverse(WB_STRESS) /4*100.
score_rules.use: Exploratory covariate only. It is not WHO-5, PHQ-4, K6, or any validated clinical measure.
score_rules.interpretation: Analyze as continuous. For clinical mental-health measurement, append an approved/licensed validated measure without modifying its wording or scoring.

score_rules.inputs: For each sensitive theme, retain thought-frequency, unwantedness, and impact. For group-level analyses: prevalence of nonzero thought-frequency and mean endorsed severity; report only aggregates.
score_rules.interpretation: No participant-facing label, no individual classification, no risk score, no access for recruiters, instructors, payment staff, or generic app administrators.

classification.purpose: Return a multi-axis descriptive research profile only; not a diagnosis, risk label, legal label, or participant-facing judgement.
classification.completeness: Do not calculate an axis unless its stated completeness rule is met. Treat 97=unfamiliar, 98=not applicable, and 99=prefer not to answer as missing, never as zero.

tags: Continuous score only; avoid labels in participant view
Aggregate research analysis only; never shown as an individual profile axis

prohibited_uses: No individual clinical diagnosis or paraphilic-disorder classification.
No risk score, likelihood-of-offending score, discipline score, hiring score, academic score, relationship-compatibility score, or insurance/benefits decision.
No automatic staff alert based only on answers. Any support resources must be participant-controlled and IRB-approved.
No merging restricted sensitive-module variables into CII, CAP, KIS, CCS, DFI, or a participant-facing profile.

routing: Render Module D as a grid/matrix with one theme per row and three initial responses: Appeal, Desire, Practice.
Offer up to five selected-theme follow-ups only for themes with Appeal, Desire, or Practice >=1.
Display Module J only after a separate opt-in; no behavioral, target, event, date, location, or free-text questions in Module J.
Allow skip/prefer-not-to-answer at every sensitive item; do not force a response to advance.
Randomize only the wording format in the method-condition field; preserve semantic content and response options across conditions.
data_separation: Store recruitment/compensation/contact data separately from survey answers and join only by a randomly generated study ID.
Use a restricted-access table for Module J. Exclude recruiters, course-instructors, payment staff, and general app administrators from access.
Disable IP-address retention, precise geolocation, free text, and third-party behavioral trackers unless specifically justified and IRB-approved.
Suppress small-cell reporting and enforce role-based access, audit logs, encryption in transit/at rest, and a retention/deletion schedule.
quality: Use QA_ATTENTION and duration/outlier rules as flags for sensitivity analysis, not automatic exclusion.
Run cognitive interviews and a pilot before setting final thresholds; preregister the scoring and missing-data rules.
Validate translations independently and test measurement invariance across key demographic and recruitment groups.

references.citation: American Psychiatric Association. Paraphilic Disorders (DSM-5 explanatory document).
Wignall, L. et al. (2025). The Kink Orientation Scale: Developing and Validating a Measure of Kink Desire, Practice, and Identity. Journal of Sex Research, 62(3), 307–316.
Winters, G. M. et al. (2023). The Development, Content Validation, and Pilot Testing of the Paraphilic Interests and Disorders Scale.
King, B. M. et al. (2022). The Influence of Social Desirability on Sexual Behavior Surveys: A Review.
Bowling, J. et al. (2024). Disclosing and Reporting of Consent Violations Among Kink Practitioners.
U.S. HHS Office for Human Research Protections. Informed Consent FAQs.
National Institutes of Health. Certificates of Confidentiality.
